description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a reference deployment for a fleet of IoT gateways.  
Create a small, self-contained deliverable that lets a systems integrator review (without elevated
privileges) the exact firewall rules that must be applied on the devices.

Required end-state of the workstation
-------------------------------------

1. Directory  
   • /home/user/deployment/firewall  
   must exist.

2. Executable shell script  
   • /home/user/deployment/firewall/iot_firewall_rules.sh  
   must exist, be marked executable, start with a POSIX she-bang (#!/bin/bash) and contain **exactly
   five** non-empty, non-comment lines **in the order shown below**:

        iptables -P INPUT DROP
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -s 192.168.1.0/24 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
        iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 1883 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT
        echo "Firewall rules applied for IoT deployment."

   (No trailing spaces after any line.)

3. Plain-text human summary  
   • /home/user/deployment/firewall/README_FIREWALL.txt  
   must exist and consist of **exactly four** lines, each describing the corresponding rule
   from the script in the following forced format (no leading or trailing whitespace):

        RULE1: Default policy set to DROP all inbound traffic
        RULE2: Allow SSH (port 22) only from 192.168.1.0/24
        RULE3: Allow HTTP (port 80) from anywhere
        RULE4: Allow MQTT (port 1883) only from 10.0.0.0/8

4. Simple progress log  
   • /home/user/deployment/firewall/setup.log  
   must exist and contain **exactly three** lines, **in this order**, each wrapped in square brackets
   and containing the fixed tokens shown (no dates/times, no extra text):

        [CREATED_SCRIPT] iot_firewall_rules.sh
        [SET_EXECUTABLE] iot_firewall_rules.sh
        [CREATED_README] README_FIREWALL.txt

What you need to do
-------------------
Operate only as the normal user “user”.

a. Create the directory structure.  
b. Build the script with the five lines shown, ensure it is executable.  
c. Write the README with the four rule descriptions in the exact format above.  
d. Record progress in setup.log using the three fixed tokens in the required order.

The automated checker will:

• Verify that the three files exist in /home/user/deployment/firewall.  
• Confirm that iot_firewall_rules.sh is mode 755 or 750 (i.e., executable).  
• Read iot_firewall_rules.sh and assert the five-line sequence matches exactly.  
• Read README_FIREWALL.txt and assert the four-line content & order.  
• Read setup.log and assert the three token lines & order.

No super-user access is involved: do **not** actually invoke iptables or ufw on the host.  
Your job is purely to compose the artefacts so that an integrator can later run the script
under root on the target IoT devices.

When finished, you should be able to list the directory:

$ ls -l /home/user/deployment/firewall
iot_firewall_rules.sh*
README_FIREWALL.txt
setup.log
truth:
Expected paths and exact contents after successful completion:

1. File: /home/user/deployment/firewall/iot_firewall_rules.sh  
   Permissions: 0755 (executable by owner at minimum)  
   Contents (including final newline, no extra blank lines):

#!/bin/bash
iptables -P INPUT DROP
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -s 192.168.1.0/24 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 1883 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT
echo "Firewall rules applied for IoT deployment."

2. File: /home/user/deployment/firewall/README_FIREWALL.txt  
   Permissions: 0644  
   Contents (including final newline):

RULE1: Default policy set to DROP all inbound traffic
RULE2: Allow SSH (port 22) only from 192.168.1.0/24
RULE3: Allow HTTP (port 80) from anywhere
RULE4: Allow MQTT (port 1883) only from 10.0.0.0/8

3. File: /home/user/deployment/firewall/setup.log  
   Permissions: 0644  
   Contents (including final newline):

[CREATED_SCRIPT] iot_firewall_rules.sh
[SET_EXECUTABLE] iot_firewall_rules.sh
[CREATED_README] README_FIREWALL.txt
